creator: ohauer@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2011-02-23 09:00, Sayetsky Anton wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/154947; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Sayetsky Anton <vsjcfm@gmail.com>
> To: bug-followup@freebsd.org
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/154947: security/nmap 5.51 cannot scan targets over MPD's
>  PPTP VPN link
> Date: Wed, 23 Feb 2011 09:53:03 +0200
> 
>  I tried to install nmap-5.50 from binary packages also. And nothing changed.
> 

Hi Anton,

It has nothing to do with the nmap version since this is how nmap is designed!

If you are able to reconfigure MPD so it displays a default IP and not a netgraph
route then I suspect you can use nmap with root privileges.

> netstat -rn | grep default
> default ng0 US 0 2809 ng0

Maybe this snipped from the nmap code explains better

file targets.cc around line 410+


Target *nexthost(HostGroupState *hs, TargetGroup *exclude_group,
 struct scan_lists *ports, int pingtype) {
...
 if (o.isr00t && o.af() == AF_INET &&
  ((pingtype &
(PINGTYPE_TCP|PINGTYPE_UDP|PINGTYPE_SCTP_INIT|PINGTYPE_PROTO|PINGTYPE_ARP))
  || o.RawScan() )) {
   t->TargetSockAddr(&ss, &sslen);
   if (!nmap_route_dst(&ss, &rnfo)) {
   fatal("%s: failed to determine route to %s", __func__, t->NameIP());
   }
....

This code is the reason you cannot use nmap as root or call a scan function
which need root privileges.
However it works as designed!

--
Regards,
olli
